---
title: "flextable Theme Implementation for Manuscript"
format: 
  html:
    code-fold: true
    toc: true
---

# flextable Manuscript Theme

This notebook provides ready-to-use `flextable` theme functions that replicate 
the styling from `theme_gt_manuscript()`. Use this as a drop-in replacement for
existing `gt` tables when Word/Google Docs compatibility is needed.

```{r setup, message=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(flextable)
library(officer)

# Install if needed:
# install.packages(c("flextable", "officer"))
```

## Theme Function Definition

```{r theme-function}
#' flextable Theme for Manuscript Tables
#'
#' Provides Cell Genomics-compliant table styling with Word/Google Docs compatibility.
#' Replicates the appearance of theme_gt_manuscript() using flextable.
#'
#' @param ft A flextable object
#' @param striped Logical: add alternating row colors (default: TRUE)
#' @param font_family Font family (default: "Roboto")
#' @param base_font_size Base font size in pt (default: 9)
#' @param header_bg Header background color (default: "#F5F5F5")
#' @param stripe_color Stripe color for alternating rows (default: "#FAFAFA")
#' @param ... Additional arguments passed to flextable functions
#'
#' @return A styled flextable object
#'
#' @export
#'
#' @examples
#' \dontrun{
#' data %>%
#'   flextable() %>%
#'   theme_flextable_manuscript()
#' }
theme_flextable_manuscript <- function(
  ft,
  striped = TRUE,
  font_family = "Roboto",
  base_font_size = 9,
  header_bg = "#F5F5F5",
  stripe_color = "#FAFAFA",
  ...
) {
  
  n_rows <- nrow(ft$body$dataset)
  
  # Apply base styling
  ft_styled <- ft %>%
    # Font styling
    font(fontname = font_family, part = "all") %>%
    fontsize(size = base_font_size, part = "body") %>%
    fontsize(size = base_font_size + 1, part = "header") %>%
    bold(part = "header") %>%
    
    # Remove default borders
    border_remove() %>%
    
    # Top and bottom borders (2px solid black)
    hline_top(
      border = fp_border(width = 2, color = "black"),
      part = "header"
    ) %>%
    hline_bottom(
      border = fp_border(width = 2, color = "black"),
      part = "body"
    ) %>%
    
    # Header bottom border (1px solid black)
    hline_bottom(
      border = fp_border(width = 1, color = "black"),
      part = "header"
    ) %>%
    
    # Alignment
    align(align = "center", part = "header") %>%
    valign(valign = "middle", part = "all") %>%
    
    # Background colors
    bg(bg = header_bg, part = "header") %>%
    
    # Padding
    padding(padding.top = 8, padding.bottom = 8, part = "all") %>%
    padding(padding.left = 10, padding.right = 10, part = "all") %>%
    
    # Table width
    set_table_properties(layout = "autofit", width = 1.0)
  
  # Add striped rows if requested
  if (striped && n_rows > 1) {
    ft_styled <- ft_styled %>%
      bg(
        i = seq(2, n_rows, by = 2),
        bg = stripe_color,
        part = "body"
      )
  }
  
  return(ft_styled)
}


#' Format flextable Numbers Like gt::fmt_integer()
#'
#' @param ft A flextable object
#' @param columns Column names or indices to format
#' @param big_mark Thousands separator (default: ",")
#'
#' @export
fmt_integer_flextable <- function(ft, columns, big_mark = ",") {
  ft %>%
    colformat_int(j = columns, big.mark = big_mark)
}


#' Format flextable Numbers Like gt::fmt_number()
#'
#' @param ft A flextable object
#' @param columns Column names or indices to format
#' @param decimals Number of decimal places
#'
#' @export
fmt_number_flextable <- function(ft, columns, decimals = 2) {
  ft %>%
    colformat_double(j = columns, digits = decimals)
}


#' Set Column Labels Like gt::cols_label()
#'
#' @param ft A flextable object
#' @param ... Named arguments: column_name = "Display Label"
#'
#' @export
cols_label_flextable <- function(ft, ...) {
  labels <- list(...)
  ft %>%
    set_header_labels(values = labels)
}
```

## Example Usage

### Sample Data

```{r sample-data}
# Create sample data matching project structure
variant_summary <- tibble::tribble(
  ~benchmark, ~ref, ~bench_type, ~total_bp, ~variant_count, ~density_per_mb,
  "v4.2.1", "GRCh38", "smvar", 2857433814, 3486393, 1.220,
  "v5.0q", "GRCh38", "smvar", 2863320957, 3512847, 1.227,
  "v5.0q", "CHM13v2.0", "smvar", 2910837291, 3548921, 1.219,
  "v4.2.1", "GRCh38", "stvar", 2857433814, 11234, 0.00393,
  "v5.0q", "GRCh38", "stvar", 2863320957, 12456, 0.00435,
  "v5.0q", "CHM13v2.0", "stvar", 2910837291, 13102, 0.00450
)
```

### Basic Table with Manuscript Theme

```{r basic-example}
variant_summary %>%
  flextable() %>%
  
  # Format numbers
  fmt_integer_flextable(columns = c("total_bp", "variant_count")) %>%
  fmt_number_flextable(columns = "density_per_mb", decimals = 4) %>%
  
  # Set readable column labels
  cols_label_flextable(
    benchmark = "Benchmark",
    ref = "Reference",
    bench_type = "Type",
    total_bp = "Total Bases",
    variant_count = "Variant Count",
    density_per_mb = "Density (per Mb)"
  ) %>%
  
  # Apply manuscript theme
  theme_flextable_manuscript() %>%
  
  # Right-align numeric columns
  align(j = c("total_bp", "variant_count", "density_per_mb"), align = "right", part = "body")
```

### Grouped Table

```{r grouped-example}
variant_summary %>%
  arrange(ref, bench_type) %>%
  flextable() %>%
  
  # Format
  fmt_integer_flextable(columns = c("total_bp", "variant_count")) %>%
  fmt_number_flextable(columns = "density_per_mb", decimals = 4) %>%
  
  # Labels
  cols_label_flextable(
    benchmark = "Benchmark",
    ref = "Reference",
    bench_type = "Type",
    total_bp = "Total Bases",
    variant_count = "Variant Count",
    density_per_mb = "Density (per Mb)"
  ) %>%
  
  # Group by reference genome
  merge_v(j = "ref") %>%
  
  # Theme
  theme_flextable_manuscript() %>%
  
  # Alignment
  align(j = c("total_bp", "variant_count", "density_per_mb"), align = "right", part = "body") %>%
  
  # Fix merged cell borders
  fix_border_issues()
```

### Customized Theme (Override Defaults)

```{r custom-theme}
variant_summary %>%
  slice(1:3) %>%
  flextable() %>%
  
  fmt_integer_flextable(columns = c("total_bp", "variant_count")) %>%
  fmt_number_flextable(columns = "density_per_mb", decimals = 4) %>%
  
  cols_label_flextable(
    benchmark = "Benchmark",
    ref = "Reference",
    bench_type = "Type",
    total_bp = "Total Bases",
    variant_count = "Variant Count",
    density_per_mb = "Density (per Mb)"
  ) %>%
  
  # Custom theme parameters
  theme_flextable_manuscript(
    striped = FALSE,          # No row striping
    font_family = "Arial",    # Different font
    base_font_size = 10,      # Larger text
    header_bg = "#E8E8E8"     # Different header color
  ) %>%
  
  align(j = c("total_bp", "variant_count", "density_per_mb"), align = "right", part = "body")
```

## Side-by-Side Comparison: gt vs flextable

### Current Approach (gt)

```{r gt-comparison, eval=FALSE}
# This is how tables currently work (HTML only)
library(gt)
source(here::here("R/plot_themes.R"))

variant_summary %>%
  gt() %>%
  fmt_integer(columns = c(total_bp, variant_count)) %>%
  fmt_number(columns = density_per_mb, decimals = 4) %>%
  cols_label(
    benchmark = "Benchmark",
    ref = "Reference",
    bench_type = "Type",
    total_bp = "Total Bases",
    variant_count = "Variant Count",
    density_per_mb = "Density (per Mb)"
  ) %>%
  theme_gt_manuscript()
```

### Proposed Approach (flextable)

```{r flextable-comparison}
# This approach works in Word and Google Docs
variant_summary %>%
  flextable() %>%
  fmt_integer_flextable(columns = c("total_bp", "variant_count")) %>%
  fmt_number_flextable(columns = "density_per_mb", decimals = 4) %>%
  cols_label_flextable(
    benchmark = "Benchmark",
    ref = "Reference",
    bench_type = "Type",
    total_bp = "Total Bases",
    variant_count = "Variant Count",
    density_per_mb = "Density (per Mb)"
  ) %>%
  theme_flextable_manuscript() %>%
  align(j = c("total_bp", "variant_count", "density_per_mb"), align = "right", part = "body")
```

## Creating Word Documents Directly

For maximum control, create Word documents programmatically:

```{r word-doc, eval=FALSE}
# Create a complete Word document with tables
doc <- read_docx() %>%
  
  # Title
  body_add_par("Variant Benchmark Summary", style = "heading 1") %>%
  body_add_par("", style = "Normal") %>%
  
  # First table
  body_add_par("Table 1: Variant Statistics by Benchmark", style = "heading 2") %>%
  body_add_flextable(
    variant_summary %>%
      flextable() %>%
      fmt_integer_flextable(columns = c("total_bp", "variant_count")) %>%
      fmt_number_flextable(columns = "density_per_mb", decimals = 4) %>%
      cols_label_flextable(
        benchmark = "Benchmark",
        ref = "Reference",
        bench_type = "Type",
        total_bp = "Total Bases",
        variant_count = "Variant Count",
        density_per_mb = "Density (per Mb)"
      ) %>%
      theme_flextable_manuscript() %>%
      align(j = c("total_bp", "variant_count", "density_per_mb"), 
            align = "right", part = "body")
  ) %>%
  
  body_add_par("", style = "Normal") %>%
  body_add_par("", style = "Normal") %>%
  
  # More content...
  body_add_par("Additional analysis and tables can be added here.", style = "Normal")

# Save
print(doc, target = "variant_summary_report.docx")
message("Word document created: variant_summary_report.docx")
```

## Migration Checklist

To replace `gt` tables with `flextable` in existing code:

1. **Copy theme function** → Add to `R/plot_themes.R` or new `R/flextable_themes.R`

2. **Update dependencies:**
   ```r
   # In R console (with renv active):
   renv::install(c("flextable", "officer"))
   renv::snapshot()
   ```

3. **Find and replace in analysis files:**
   - `gt::gt()` → `flextable()`
   - `gt::fmt_integer()` → `fmt_integer_flextable()`
   - `gt::fmt_number()` → `fmt_number_flextable()`
   - `gt::cols_label()` → `cols_label_flextable()`
   - `theme_gt_manuscript()` → `theme_flextable_manuscript()`
   - Add alignment: `%>% align(j = numeric_cols, align = "right", part = "body")`

4. **Update Quarto YAML:**
   ```yaml
   format:
     html: default
     docx: default  # Now produces properly formatted Word output
   ```

5. **Test rendering:**
   ```r
   quarto::quarto_render("analysis/file.qmd", output_format = "all")
   ```

6. **Verify Word export:**
   - Open `.docx` file
   - Check formatting (fonts, borders, colors, alignment)
   - Upload to Google Drive → Open with Google Docs

## When to Use Each Package

**Use flextable when:**
- Tables need to go in Word documents
- Tables will be copied to Google Docs
- You need Office (Word/PowerPoint) integration
- Cross-format consistency is required

**Use gt when:**
- Output is HTML only (website, R Markdown HTML)
- You need interactive features (gtsummary integration)
- Maximum aesthetic control for web display

**Hybrid approach:**
- Keep `gt` for HTML-only outputs (supplementary materials, lab website)
- Use `flextable` for manuscript tables and reports that need Word/Google Docs compatibility
